plan: A3 Train on Fire at Platform

critical_steps.task: Confirm incident location (with TC if appropriate). If TC instructs, Station Evacuation may be triggered before arrival of the incident train.
Arrange to use a portable fire extinguisher to put out the fire if safe to do so.
Check the status of Fire Protection System and trigger the fire alarm bells/siren if necessary.
Ensure the fire pump has started automatically or arrange a manual start if necessary.
Locate the incident location and observe the platform condition via CCTV.
Arrange Station Staff escorting FSD officials with cue card within 6 mins.
Inform all staff members to switch HPR to line mode.
Conduct detrainment and open all Platform Doors after confirming with TC.
Activate proper smoke extraction/emergency ventilation mode after communication with ESC (Fire in tunnel).
Activate Station Evacuation if required, and request Emergency Services through TC if necessary. Confirm train service pattern, interchange arrangement and Platform arrangement with TC.
Provide or arrange assistance to disabled persons, elderly, or passengers in need.
Warn other persons to keep away from the incident site.
Arrange to use a portable fire extinguisher to put out the fire if safe to do so.
Operate ESS/PEP to prevent train operation in Auto/CM mode if necessary.
Switch off air-conditioning inside SCR to avoid infiltration of smoke, if necessary.
Summon assistance from FMD, CSRPU via TC or request manpower backup.
Arrange staff to manually switch off/close the Town Gas/LPG supply, if necessary.
Inform TC for any station equipment damage.
Put on escape hood if necessary.
Confirm the activation of Station Evacuation Mode on IBP/SMS/MCS/DIBP/ILCP.
Deploy staff(s) to carry out the evacuation according to the contingency plan. Assign Casualty Liaison Officer if multiple injuries are found.
Open Manual Service Gates/Emergency Service Gate/Emergency Escape Gates/Emergency Exit Gates/Emergency Exit Door to accelerate evacuation process.
Change running direction of Escalators/Travelators, or stop them as fixed staircases (Emergency Brake MUST be applied).
Identify passengers who may have difficulties in evacuation and provide assistance.
Ensure all lifts are stopped at the unaffected landing with nobody trapped.
Direct passengers to leave the station.
Cordon off the incident site.
Inform working parties and staff at Back of House area for emergency evacuation, then report at designated assembly point.
Inform all tenants to evacuate and report at designated assembly point.
Inform the annexed Buildings' Management Offices.
Crosscheck station equipment for evacuation readiness: PIDS, PA, AFC Gates, Emergency Signage.
Crosscheck with ESC to make sure proper ECS Mode has been operated.
Crosscheck Escalators/Travelators are running in proper direction according to contingency plan.
Crosscheck Manual Service Gates/Emergency Service Gate/Emergency Escape Gates/Emergency Exit Door are opened.
Check if Platform, Concourse, Subway are cleared of passengers and report result.
Close all entrances/subways to prevent inflow of passengers after evacuation and post Station Close notices.
Report to SC/IO after confirming entrances are closed.
Set up Incident Control Post at proper location.
Take up IO duty before DSM arrival.
Appoint an IOA.
Set up a temporary telephone line at the ICP.
Deploy staff to escort emergency services to the incident site.
Set up the access route.
Set up the egress route.
Head-count personnel of tenants and working parties at assembly point and report to IO.
Operate ECS Mode after evacuation to drain away smoke if platform doors are installed.
